title: Thrift & Ride
description: Campaign concept and art direction for the launch of the adidas Forum '84 'Forum by New York City' colorway. A repurposed vintage NYC transit bus, wrapped in a custom Forum livery and outfitted as a mobile thrift shop, served as the campaign's hero environment — connecting the silhouette's basketball-court heritage to a contemporary NYC street and secondhand-style culture. Photography captured the bus exterior, styled interior, product, packaging, and a cast of New York creatives shopping the rolling thrift floor. [NEEDS REVIEW]

caseStudy.problem: [NEEDS REVIEW] Placeholder brief in notes.txt — confirm the campaign objective. Working assumption: launch a heritage-court silhouette into a contemporary NYC streetwear context without leaning on the usual NBA-throwback playbook, and give the colorway a tangible cultural moment beyond a standard product drop.
caseStudy.process: [NEEDS REVIEW] Built the campaign around a single hero object — a wrapped vintage city bus styled as a rolling Forum-branded thrift shop. Developed the bus livery, interior set design, custom packaging (hangtag, dust bag, instax-ready insert), branded tote, lookbook, and cast styling. Directed a photo shoot on 2021-06-28 capturing the bus on-site at a graffiti-covered warehouse, the styled interior, on-foot street moments, and product/packaging flatlays.
caseStudy.outcome: [NEEDS REVIEW] Confirm launch metrics, press coverage, awards, and any retail/sell-through outcomes. Two finished video edits exist (16:9 30MB and 16:9 YouTube cuts) — flag for inclusion once schema supports video.

title: ping.com
description: Complete website design for PING, the premium golf equipment manufacturer. A product-first commerce platform structured around the Clubs, Accessories, and Juniors categories, with detailed PDPs surfacing technical specifications, shaft and grip configuration, and customer reviews. Editorial hero moments feature PING's tour professional roster. Fully responsive across desktop and mobile. Live at https://ping.com/en-us.

title: rockhall.com
description: Full website redesign for the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame — the inductees Hall-of-Fame universe, the Cleveland museum's visitor experience, and the editorial content program. The system centers on the 2025 induction class and the Watch-The-Induction livestream, with sub-systems for A–Z and Class-of inductee browsing, long-form story pages, and an RRTV motion identity that threads video reels across the site. Bold yellow-on-black display typography pairs with archival rock photography; the museum sub-brand carries a distinct blue accent system. Live at https://rockhall.com/.
